majorFacilities.name: Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Braintree
Atrius Health / Harvard Vanguard Medical Associates Braintree
South Shore Health Health Express Braintree
majorFacilities.type: urgent care with on-site x-ray and lab

dominantFacilityType: rehabilitation hospital and large multi-specialty outpatient offices
notes: No acute-care hospital in Braintree. New England Sinai Hospital, often associated with Braintree because of the 'Braintree Rehabilitation' brand, was in STOUGHTON and closed permanently in April 2024. Nearest acute hospital is South Shore Hospital in Weymouth.

dominantFacilityType: ambulatory surgery and outpatient physician offices
notes: No hospital in Shrewsbury. Some directories list the UMass Memorial Memorial Campus with a Shrewsbury address; that campus is in Worcester.

majorFacilities.name: MetroWest Medical Center — Leonard Morse Hospital

notes: MAJOR STATUS CHANGE: do not describe Leonard Morse as a general or acute hospital. Its emergency department closed in October 2020, and in February 2025 MetroWest filed to discontinue the remaining med/surg unit, ICU and operating rooms, converting the campus fully to behavioral health.

majorFacilities.name: Lowell General Hospital — Chelmsford outpatient campus
Surgery Center of the Merrimack Valley
Tufts Medicine Pediatric Specialty Center Chelmsford

dominantFacilityType: hospital-affiliated outpatient campus with ambulatory surgery
notes: No hospital licensed in Chelmsford itself. Drum Hill is a genuine long-standing local district name, not coined.

majorFacilities.name: Beth Israel Lahey Health Care Center — Watertown
majorFacilities.type: multispecialty outpatient center with urgent care

notes: No hospital in Watertown. The BILH multispecialty site opened August 2024 and added urgent care in May 2025 — new, growing infrastructure. Nearest hospital is Mount Auburn in Cambridge.

dominantFacilityType: independent primary care and small private practices
notes: Genuinely low. No hospital, no ambulatory surgery centre, no confirmed freestanding dialysis. Residents use Milton, Braintree and Brockton facilities.

majorFacilities.name: Beth Israel Lahey Health Primary Care — Hartwell Avenue

notes: No hospital in Lexington. Hartwell Avenue hosts at least two distinct system-run medical buildings. Lahey's outpatient center is on Hayden Avenue, a separate road.

healthSystems: UMass Memorial Health (Milford Regional Medical Center)
dominantFacilityType: hospital-affiliated urgent care and outpatient diagnostics
notes: No hospital in Franklin. Everything confirmed sits in a single satellite building on West Central Street. Milford Regional was acquired by UMass Memorial Health on 1 October 2024.

dominantFacilityType: community hospital with affiliated outpatient practices
notes: Open and stable. 73 beds, named among U.S. News Best Hospitals for 2025-2026.

DO NOT LIST NORWOOD HOSPITAL AS OPERATING. It has been closed since a June 2020 flood; the rebuild halted in February 2024, the state revoked its Determination of Need in autumn 2024, and as of January 2026 the site is a sealed shell with no operator. Norwood is a market that LOST its hospital.

majorFacilities.name: UMass Memorial Health — Milford Regional Medical Center

notes: RENAME: since the 1 October 2024 acquisition the hospital is formally 'UMass Memorial Health — Milford Regional Medical Center'.

majorFacilities.name: Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital — Milton

notes: Open and stable, 100 beds. Quincy Medical Center in the neighbouring town closed in 2014, which increased Milton's catchment.

majorFacilities.name: Salem Hospital Blood Draw Lab — Saugus

notes: Genuinely low. Union Hospital in neighbouring Lynn closed and was demolished, so Saugus residents travel to Salem or Melrose.

McLean treats over 9,000 inpatients annually and has been the top-ranked freestanding psychiatric hospital in the United States for twenty consecutive years. There is no general acute hospital in Belmont.

majorFacilities.name: Lahey Hospital & Medical Center

notes: Highest-density town in the region. Open and actively expanding: an April 2026 Determination of Need covers roughly $79.5M for 36 new med/surg beds and a new cardiac lab, because the campus runs above typical capacity.

No assisted living or memory care in Shirley. RiverCourt Residences markets to Shirley but is physically in West Groton.
No assisted living or memory care in Harvard.
No assisted living or memory care in Berlin. The Emerson urgent care at Highland Common East is branded Hudson; Highland Commons straddles the Berlin/Hudson line.
Not a licensed assisted living residence — it is the town's elderly housing complex. No assisted living or memory care in Bolton.
Zero senior living communities with a Boxborough address.
Nothing found in Carlisle.
Everything branded 'Boylston' is elsewhere — The Residence at Boylston Place is in Brookline, and the nursing and assisted living stock is in West Boylston, Shrewsbury and Worcester.
Mill Pond Rest Home is listed by several directories at a Sherborn address; it is actually in ASHLAND. The Sherborn address belongs to a home-care agency, not a residential facility.
Nearest facilities are in Leominster and Gardner. 'Princeton Transitional Care' results refer to Princeton, NEW JERSEY.
All 'Dover' results are either surrounding towns or Dover, NEW HAMPSHIRE — Wentworth-Douglass and Dover Family Practice are in New Hampshire.
